usage_rule: Use these records only to create SCREEN-* observations and human-review questions. A match, a missing match, or a repair does not establish a WCAG or JIS outcome without the registered criterion procedure and target-specific human evidence.

patterns.title: Missing or inconsistent default page language
patterns.trigger: The page has no programmatically determinable default human language, or the declared language appears inconsistent with the page as a whole.
patterns.observation: Record the declared document language, the dominant visible language, and the inspected target state without deciding the criterion outcome.
human_check: Determine the default human language of the page.
Compare it with the programmatic document-language declaration and current rendered state.
patterns.remediation: Set or correct the document-level language using the mechanism supported by the content technology and preserve it in templates.
patterns.retest: Inspect the rendered document language and confirm it matches the page-wide default language; assess language changes within parts separately.
patterns.claim_boundary: Attribute presence alone is not a result; the declared value must match the page's default human language.

patterns.title: Duplicate identifier values
patterns.trigger: Two or more elements expose the same identifier in one document.
patterns.observation: Record every duplicate value and the relationships, labels, controls, or script references that use it.
human_check: Determine whether duplication changes a component name, role, value, state, relationship, or operation.
Do not map duplication to a criterion solely because the identifier is repeated.
patterns.remediation: Assign unique identifiers and update every dependent reference without changing the intended relationship or behavior.
patterns.retest: Repeat identifier inspection and exercise each affected label, control, fragment, and scripted behavior.
patterns.claim_boundary: A duplicate identifier is a code-quality signal; it is not automatically a failure of SC 4.1.2 or another criterion.

patterns.title: Fragment link target is missing or not reached
patterns.trigger: An in-page link refers to a target that is absent, unreachable, or implemented through behavior that static inspection cannot resolve.
patterns.observation: Record the link, requested fragment, rendered target state, and actual focus or scroll result.
human_check: Activate the mechanism in the rendered application.
Determine whether it is intended to bypass a repeated block and whether focus reaches a meaningful target.
patterns.remediation: Restore the intended target or remove the obsolete link, and manage focus when the application uses a scripted equivalent.
patterns.retest: Activate the link using a keyboard and verify target existence, visible movement, and meaningful focus behavior.
patterns.claim_boundary: A missing fragment can break a bypass mechanism, but only the repeated-block context and rendered behavior establish relevance to SC 2.4.1.

patterns.title: Image has no text-alternative mechanism
patterns.trigger: An image or image control has no detectable text-alternative mechanism.
patterns.observation: Record the image, computed name, nearby equivalent text, visible purpose, and interactive state.
human_check: Classify the non-text content purpose.
Determine whether an equivalent alternative, empty alternative, or exception is appropriate in context.
patterns.remediation: Provide an alternative that serves the same purpose, or intentionally hide decorative content from assistive technology.
patterns.retest: Compare the visible purpose with the computed alternative and any longer equivalent text in the same state.
patterns.claim_boundary: Missing alt syntax is a candidate only; purpose, context, and exceptions require human review.

patterns.title: Adjacent image and text may repeat the same accessible wording
patterns.trigger: An image alternative and adjacent text within the same control or link appear to repeat the same wording.
patterns.observation: Record the computed accessible name, visible label, destination or action, and whether both pieces contribute distinct meaning.
human_check: Listen to or inspect the complete accessible name in context.
Determine whether repetition is redundant or whether each phrase conveys necessary distinct information.
patterns.remediation: Remove only the redundant contribution, commonly by using an empty alternative for a decorative image beside equivalent link text.
patterns.retest: Inspect the final computed name and confirm that the control purpose remains complete without duplicate wording.
patterns.claim_boundary: String equality does not prove harmful repetition; the full accessible name and purpose must be reviewed.

patterns.title: Input control lacks a usable label or instruction association
patterns.trigger: A user-input control has no detectable associated label, name, or instruction mechanism.
patterns.observation: Record the control type, visible prompt, computed name, helper text, required state, and expected input format.
human_check: Determine whether the control requests user input and whether its purpose and necessary instructions are available before input.
Review programmatic association and visible clarity separately.
patterns.remediation: Provide a persistent visible label and programmatic association, plus instructions needed to complete the input.
patterns.retest: Inspect the computed name and instructions, then complete the field using keyboard and assistive-technology-facing information.
patterns.claim_boundary: An accessible name alone may not supply the visible label or instructions required for the task.

patterns.title: Related form controls may lack a programmatic group description
patterns.trigger: Related radio buttons, checkboxes, or repeated controls appear visually grouped without an equivalent programmatic group and description.
patterns.observation: Record group boundaries, the shared question or instruction, each control label, names, and rendered accessibility relationships.
human_check: Determine the intended group from the visible task.
Compare it with fieldset and legend, group roles, or another supported programmatic relationship.
patterns.remediation: Represent the actual group and its description programmatically without combining unrelated controls.
patterns.retest: Navigate the controls in sequence and confirm the shared context and individual labels are available.
patterns.claim_boundary: Matching name values or DOM ancestry alone do not establish the intended human-facing group.

patterns.title: Data cells may not expose the intended header relationships
patterns.trigger: A data table has header cells whose relationship to data cells is absent, ambiguous, or more complex than a simple header arrangement.
patterns.observation: Record the visible table structure, header levels, spans, computed relationships, and representative data cells.
human_check: Determine the actual row and column relationships from the data.
Confirm each representative data cell exposes the headers needed to interpret it.
patterns.remediation: Use table markup and supported header-association mechanisms that represent the real data relationships.
patterns.retest: Inspect or announce representative cells across every header level and compare the exposed headers with the visible table.
patterns.claim_boundary: The presence or absence of a scope attribute alone does not determine whether every required relationship is available.

patterns.title: Embedded frame lacks a descriptive programmatic name
patterns.trigger: An embedded frame has no detectable programmatic name or has a name that does not distinguish its content or purpose.
patterns.observation: Record the frame source, visible context, computed name, repeated-frame context, and whether the frame is user-facing.
human_check: Determine the frame purpose and whether its name distinguishes it from other frames.
Confirm the relevant criterion from current sources instead of inheriting a historical mapping automatically.
patterns.remediation: Provide a concise descriptive frame name and keep it aligned with the embedded content or remove an obsolete frame.
patterns.retest: Inspect the computed frame name and navigate among frames in the rendered page.
patterns.claim_boundary: A historical technique association is not a direct criterion result; frame purpose, navigation context, and current WCAG sources must be reviewed.

patterns.title: Repeated content may lack an effective bypass mechanism
patterns.trigger: A page appears to repeat navigation or other blocks before primary content, while no effective bypass mechanism is evident from the available inspection.
patterns.observation: Record the page-set scope, repeated blocks, sequential navigation cost, candidate landmarks or headings, and any skip control behavior.
human_check: Confirm the content is repeated across a set of pages.
Use sequential navigation to determine whether at least one effective bypass mechanism is available.
patterns.remediation: Provide an effective keyboard-accessible bypass through a skip link, appropriate regions, headings, or another supported mechanism.
patterns.retest: Repeat the task across representative pages and verify the mechanism reaches the intended content before the repeated block.
patterns.claim_boundary: Absence of a skip link is not automatically a failure because other effective bypass mechanisms may satisfy the criterion.

patterns.title: Text resizing behavior requires a 200 percent functional check
patterns.trigger: Static style inspection finds fixed or constrained text sizing, or an automated check reports no issue but actual 200 percent behavior has not been exercised.
patterns.observation: Record the browser, viewport, resize mechanism, initial and final scale, and any clipped, obscured, overlapping, or unavailable content or control.
human_check: Increase text to 200 percent without assistive technology.
Exercise essential content and functions and distinguish text resizing from separate reflow requirements.
patterns.remediation: Adjust layout and text containers so enlarged text remains available and operable; do not treat a CSS unit choice by itself as the defect.
patterns.retest: Repeat the scoped task at 200 percent and record content and functionality loss, including overlays and dialogs.
patterns.claim_boundary: Fixed units are not automatically a failure, and no automated signal is not a pass; the rendered 200 percent behavior requires human review.
